qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Non-constant-time HMAC signature comparison in JWS verification enables timing side-channel attack
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes incomplete comparison (CWE-1023) in getRandomBigIntegerZeroToMax/getRandomBigIntegerMinToMax in src/crypto-1.1.js, where incorrect compareTo checks bias DSA nonces for private key recovery. The finding describes a non-constant-time == comparison (CWE-208) at src/jws-3.3.js:489 for HMAC verification, enabling HMAC secret recovery through timing analysis. These are different files, different functions, different root causes (incorrect range validation vs timing-leaking equality check), and different vulnerability classes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes incorrect compareTo checks in getRandomBigIntegerZeroToMax and getRandomBigIntegerMinToMax in src/crypto-1.1.js that bias DSA nonces (CWE-1023). The only qualifying finding (hmac_timing_side_channel) describes a different vulnerability in a different file (src/jws-3.3.js:489, CWE-208 timing side-channel in HMAC comparison).
evidence: Advisory root cause: incorrect compareTo checks in getRandomBigIntegerZeroToMax/getRandomBigIntegerMinToMax in src/crypto-1.1.js (CWE-1023: Incomplete Comparison with Missing Factors)
hmac_timing_side_channel root cause: non-constant-time == at src/jws-3.3.js:489 (CWE-208: Observable Timing Discrepancy) — different file, function, and vulnerability class
